title: QUIC RFC 9000 STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED Runtime Emission Work Item
summary: Add direct protected-packet proof and permanent benchmark coverage for active-runtime `STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED` advisory emission when a stream write is blocked by stream-level flow-control credit.
planned_changes:
- Multi-tag the existing protected-packet write proof so the stream-level blocked-signal requirements point at a real outbound STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED packet.
- Keep negative runtime coverage proving an in-flight ack-eliciting packet suppresses duplicate blocked-signal emission while the write still fails honestly for lack of credit.
- Confirm the protected advisory packet remains retained by the sender/recovery ledger and can be retransmitted after loss.
- Add a permanent BenchmarkDotNet case for the stream-state helper branch that publishes `STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED`.
- Add focused architecture and verification artifacts for the bounded `STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED` runtime emission slice.
verification_plan: Run the focused RFC 9000 S3P1/S4P1/S4P2/S13P3/S19P13 requirement-home tests, run the STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED frame-codec fuzz coverage, run the `QuicConnectionStreamStateBenchmarks` BenchmarkDotNet suite with the Dry job, render/check the touched SpecTrace artifacts, and preserve evidence under `artifacts/verification/20260425-rfc9000-stream-data-blocked-runtime/`.
out_of_scope: Waiting asynchronously for later stream-level flow-control credit.
completion_notes: Completed as a proof and benchmark topoff over the existing runtime `STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED` branch; no production behavior changed.
